dataVerification.invariants: 24 — manually verified. CAUTION: grep -c 'name:' returns 26 (false positives from interface line + hostname). Use grep '^\s*name:' or manual enumeration.

active.title: v2.9→v2.10 announcement content (scope: expanded)
active.note: Assigned to marketing-launch-agent. Key messages: (1) default mode guide→monitor — 'monitor first, enforce when ready' adoption story; (2) wildcard policy fix security CTA; (3) 5 governed drivers (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Goose). Current version: v2.10.3.

newIssuesTriaged.title: Containment engineering post — computer-use governance narrative
newIssuesTriaged.recommendation: Elevate to P0. Anthropic announcement provides the hook. Proof points (139 agents, 1,170 PRs, 24 invariants) are compelling. Should publish within days, not weeks. Assign to marketing-launch-agent alongside or instead of #1260.

newIssuesTriaged.title: CLAW-10 scorecard — AgentGuard vs raw OpenClaw
newIssuesTriaged.recommendation: Strong credibility play. Each score already has code evidence. Good follow-on to #1387 — scorecard as supporting asset for the containment engineering post.

newIssuesTriaged.title: Meta incident demo — governance survives context compaction
newIssuesTriaged.recommendation: Conference demo must be built before May 6. Demo script defined in issue. Summer Yue incident makes the prompt-vs-kernel safety argument concrete and timely.

productInsights: Default enforcement mode changed guide→monitor (v2.10.x). New installs now observe before enforcing — reduces onboarding friction. Strong 'try before you commit' adoption angle.
Product at v2.10.3. Sprint was targeting v2.9.x; updated to v2.9→v2.10 arc to cover default mode change and all intervening features.
Capture funnel (PR #1388) is live. README now has GitHub Stars badge, Get Started section, cloud/Discussions CTAs above the fold.
Build-time stat sync script recommended for 4th consecutive cycle — stat drift is systemic. Should be filed as an engineering issue with explicit owner.
OpenCode supported in identity system (OPENCODE_HOME detection, identity.ts) but not mentioned in site's Supported Frameworks section.
Goose uses MCP server protocol (not hook-based) — different integration model from the 4 hook-based drivers (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI).

dogfoodReport.status: clean — no governance issues this run. git add/commit/push, gh pr merge, gh pr create all executed without denial. Site-only writes clean.
